question.text: A call option on USD/INR gives the buyer the right to:

answer.text: Buy USD at a predetermined exchange rate
Sell USD at a predetermined exchange rate
Buy INR at a predetermined exchange rate
Exchange USD for any other currency

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A call option gives the right to buy the underlying (base currency). For USD/INR, a call gives the right to buy USD at the strike price. A put would give the right to sell USD. **The Trap:** A put option (not call) gives the right to sell the base currency. Buying USD is equivalent to selling INR. **Cross-Exam Context:** Call/put option definitions are fundamental to Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Call on USD/INR = Right to buy USD = Right to sell INR. Put on USD/INR = Right to sell USD = Right to buy INR.
